skill.name: gwas-database
skill.description: Query the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog to retrieve SNP-trait associations, study metadata, and (when available) summary statistics when you need evidence for a variant, trait/disease, gene, or genomic region.
skill.summary: Query the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog to retrieve SNP-trait associations, study metadata, and summary statistics for genetic variants, traits, and genomic regions.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 260 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are Markdown code formatting for REST API endpoint examples, not shell commands. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'Hardcoded URL' flags reference legitimate public API URLs at ebi.ac.uk (European Bioinformatics Institute). The 'System reconnaissance' and 'HTTP library' flags correspond to standard requests.get() calls to the public GWAS Catalog API, which is the documented purpose of this scientific research skill. The 'filesystem' flag is a user-initiated download example. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential theft, or malicious patterns were found. This skill passed a prior independent audit with 'Production Ready' status and all security gates marked PASS.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick formatting misinterpreted as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 176 instances of backtick characters in Markdown as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are REST API endpoint path patterns formatted with Markdown inline code syntax (e.g., `GET /studies/{accessionId}`). No actual shell execution occurs. This is a false positive caused by pattern-matching the backtick character without semantic understanding of Markdown documentation.

low_findings.title: Legitimate HTTP requests to public scientific API
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 27 instances of HTTP library usage and 7 instances of 'System reconnaissance'. These are standard requests.get() calls to the public NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog API at ebi.ac.uk. The skill is explicitly designed to query this public scientific database. No credentials are exfiltrated, no suspicious domains are contacted, and proper rate limiting is implemented.

low_findings.title: Example URLs referencing legitimate scientific database
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 22 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' and 21 'Hardcoded URL' instances. All flagged URLs reference the legitimate NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog at ebi.ac.uk (e.g., https://www.ebi.ac.uk/gwas/rest/api). These are required endpoint constants for the skill's documented function and point to a well-known public research database operated by the European Bioinformatics Institute.

low_findings.title: Local file download example to user-specified directory
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 1 instance of 'Python file write/append' at line 773 of api_reference.md. This is part of an optional function to download GWAS summary statistics files from the public EBI FTP server. The file is written to a user-specified local output directory using a standard `with open()` pattern. No system files, configuration files, or hidden locations are targeted.

content.user_title: Explore GWAS Catalog data for variant-trait associations
content.value_statement: Researchers need quick access to curated genetic association data from published genome-wide association studies. This skill queries the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog API to retrieve SNP-trait associations, study metadata, and summary statistics for any variant, trait, gene, or genomic region.
actual_capabilities: Look up SNP-trait associations by rsID with p-values, effect sizes, and odds ratios
Search variants by EFO trait ID to find disease-associated genetic variants
Retrieve study-level metadata including cohort ancestry, sample size, and publication details
Query variants within a genomic region using chromosome and base-pair coordinates
Find gene-centric GWAS hits by HGNC gene symbol near genes of interest
Access summary statistics for fine-mapping, colocalization, and PRS development
limitations: Does not perform statistical analysis or compute new genetic associations beyond what the GWAS Catalog provides
Requires internet access to reach the GWAS Catalog REST API and Summary Statistics API
Summary statistics are only available for studies that have submitted complete genome-wide data
API rate limits apply; large bulk queries should use the FTP download option instead

use_cases.title: Variant Lookup for Clinical Genetics
use_cases.description: A clinical geneticist needs to check if a patient's novel variant lies near known GWAS hits for the phenotype under investigation. They provide an rsID and receive all reported associations with p-values, effect sizes, and study references.

use_cases.title: Disease Association Cataloging
use_cases.description: A researcher compiling evidence for a grant application needs all genome-wide significant associations for type 2 diabetes. The skill queries the catalog by EFO trait ID and returns a structured table of associated variants with significance levels.

use_cases.title: Genomic Region Characterization
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician analyzing a QTL study needs to identify which GWAS variants fall within a specific chromosome band. The skill queries the catalog by chromosomal coordinates and returns all variants with association data.

prompt_templates.title: Simple variant lookup by rsID
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GWAS Database skill to look up variant rs7903146. Retrieve all reported trait associations including p-values, effect sizes (odds ratio or beta), and the strongest allele for each association.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a specific variant of interest and needs all its known trait associations.

prompt_templates.title: Trait-based disease association search
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the GWAS Database for all genome-wide significant associations (p < 5e-8) for EFO trait EFO_0001360 (type 2 diabetes). Return results as a table with rsID, p-value, strongest allele, odds ratio, and study accession. Paginate through all available results.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs a complete list of significant associations for a specific disease or trait.

prompt_templates.title: Gene-centric variant exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GWAS Database to find all GWAS variants near the APOE gene. Retrieve the associated traits, p-values, and study metadata. Summarize which traits have the strongest evidence for association with this gene region.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher investigating a gene of interest wants to know its known GWAS associations.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-referencing variant pleiotropy across studies
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the GWAS Database for variant rs7903146 and retrieve all distinct trait associations. Then retrieve the study metadata (publication info, sample size, ancestry) for each associated study. Present a summary showing which traits are replicated across multiple independent studies and the strength of evidence for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs to assess pleiotropic effects and replication evidence for a variant.

output_examples.input: What traits are associated with variant rs7903146?
output_examples.output: The variant rs7903146 (located in the TCF7L2 gene on chromosome 10) is associated with multiple traits including type 2 diabetes (p=1.2e-30, odds ratio=1.37), fasting glucose levels (p=1.8e-15), and BMI-adjusted waist circumference (p=3.4e-9). These associations come from multiple independent studies (GCST001795, GCST003424) with European and multi-ancestry cohorts.

output_examples.input: Find all genome-wide significant associations for Alzheimer disease using EFO ID EFO_0000249
output_examples.output: The query returned 42 genome-wide significant associations for Alzheimer disease. The top variants include several in the APOE region (rs429358, p=5.2e-189), BIN1 (rs6733839, p=4.1e-15), CLU (rs9331896, p=1.1e-10), and PICALM (rs10792832, p=1.3e-8). Effect sizes, risk alleles, and study accessions are provided for each association.

output_examples.input: Get study metadata for GWAS study GCST001795
output_examples.output: Study GCST001795: 'Meta-analysis of type 2 diabetes in European populations' published in Nature Genetics (PMID: 22885924). The discovery stage included 12,171 cases and 56,862 controls across multiple European cohorts. Ancestry is predominantly European. Initial genotyping used Illumina arrays with imputation to the HapMap reference panel. Replication included 34,412 cases and 59,925 controls. Summary statistics are not available for this study.

best_practices: Add small delays (0.1-0.5 seconds) between consecutive API requests to respect public API rate limits
Cache query results locally to avoid redundant network calls during iterative multi-query analysis
Use pagination with page and size parameters to retrieve complete results, and check for empty result sets as a stop condition
anti_patterns: Making rapid consecutive requests without delays, which may trigger rate limiting or service disruption
Parsing p-value text directly without converting to numeric type, leading to incorrect threshold filtering
Performing statistical or clinical interpretation of results beyond what the GWAS Catalog provides as curated data

faq.question: What is the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog?
faq.answer: It is a curated database of all published genome-wide association studies, maintained by the European Bioinformatics Institute (EBI). It contains SNP-trait associations, study metadata, and summary statistics.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to access the GWAS Catalog?
faq.answer: No. The GWAS Catalog APIs are open access and do not require authentication or API keys.

faq.question: What is an EFO trait ID and how do I find one?
faq.answer: EFO (Experimental Factor Ontology) provides standardized identifiers for traits and diseases. You can search for EFO terms at the EBI Ontology Lookup Service.

faq.question: Can this skill access summary statistics data?
faq.answer: Yes, when summary statistics are available for a study. The skill can query the dedicated Summary Statistics API and reference the FTP download directory for bulk access.

faq.question: What file formats does the GWAS Catalog use for summary statistics?
faq.answer: Summary statistics are provided as gzipped TSV files. Harmonised files follow a standard column format across all studies.

faq.question: What is the difference between the REST API and Summary Statistics API?
faq.answer: The REST API provides curated SNP-trait associations with study metadata. The Summary Statistics API provides full genome-wide results for studies that submitted complete data.
